title: BlackRock® Canada Announces Final May Cash Distributions for the iShares® Premium Money Market ETF
summary: BlackRock Asset Management Canada Limited announced the final May 2026 cash distributions for the iShares Premium Money Market ETF (CMR). Unitholders of record on May 21, 2026, will receive a cash distribution of $0.077 per unit, payable on May 29, 2026. This announcement provides key financial details for investors in the premium money market ETF.

title: Black Rock Coffee (NASDAQ: BRCB) reshapes founder proxy and Cynosure voting deals
summary: Black Rock Coffee Bar, Inc. (BRCB) has announced significant changes to its governance and shareholder agreements. The company has entered into an irrevocable proxy with founder-related entities, giving BRCB, its CEO, or a designee authority to vote all common shares held by these entities for at least two years from May 15, 2026. Additionally, the company amended its Registration Rights Agreement, increasing demand registration requests for Cynosure Investors from three to four, and terminated a voting agreement with Cynosure Investors related to co-founder board nominations.

title: Larry Fink’s Big Payday Gets Lukewarm Reception From BlackRock Shareholders
summary: BlackRock shareholders showed lukewarm support for CEO Larry Fink's $37.7 million compensation package and those of other top executives for 2025. Approximately 65% of votes approved the compensation plans, a slight decrease from the previous year, which is below the average 90% support for "say-on-pay" proposals at S&P 500 companies. This indicates a recent decline in investor enthusiasm for BlackRock's executive pay.

title: BlackRock Declares Quarterly Dividend of $5.73 on Common Stock
summary: BlackRock, Inc. announced that its Board of Directors has declared a quarterly cash dividend of $5.73 per share of common stock. This dividend will be payable on June 23, 2026, to shareholders of record as of the close of business on June 5, 2026. The announcement was made in New York, reinforcing BlackRock's commitment to shareholder returns.

title: Adtran Holdings, Inc.: Release according to Article 40, Section 1 of the WpHG [the German Securities Trading Act] with the objective of Europe-wide distribution
summary: BlackRock, Inc. has notified Adtran Holdings, Inc. of a change in major holdings, triggered by an acquisition/disposal of shares with voting rights and a voluntary group notification. As of May 15, 2026, BlackRock's total voting rights in Adtran Holdings, Inc. are 10.97%, comprising 6.42% attached to shares and 4.56% through various instruments. This reflects a slight decrease from the previous notification's total of 11.09%.

title: Fidelity Highlights Sterling Infrastructure as AI-beneficiary
summary: Fidelity's Growth Strategies Fund performed well in Q1 2026, largely due to its significant overweight in the industrials sector, with Sterling Infrastructure (NASDAQ: STRL) being a major contributor (+33% gain). This outperformance is seen as a result of a market rotation away from AI-exposed growth stocks towards more defensive industrial assets. The article advises monitoring future Fidelity filings and STRL's performance to determine if the stock's gains are due to fundamental momentum or narrative-driven revaluation.

title: IBM Institutional Holders, Ownership Changes & Insider Trades
summary: This article details the top institutional holders of International Business Machines Corp (IBM), outlining their percentage of ownership, share changes, and filing dates. It also provides a table of recent insider transactions, including awards to directors, emphasizing the financial movements within the company's stock by major institutional investors and insiders.

title: The Powerful Signal Vanguard Is Sending to Investors
summary: Vanguard has cut fund fees for two consecutive years, totaling an estimated $600 million in foregone revenue, a move Morningstar attributes to their strong financial performance and commitment to their mission. While these cuts aren't life-changing for individual investors, they signal Vanguard's stability and are made possible by substantial organic flows and asset appreciation. This action hasn't sparked a wider fee war among competitors as much as in the past, largely because fees are already very low across the industry and investors are unlikely to switch funds over marginal differences.

title: BlackRock, Inc.'s long position in H-shares of TravelSky Technology increases to 12.51% on May 14 from 11.84% - HKEX
summary: BlackRock, Inc. has increased its long position in the H-shares of TravelSky Technology from 11.84% to 12.51% as of May 14. This change in ownership was reported through the Hong Kong Stock Exchange (HKEX). TravelSky Technology is a Chinese company that provides information technology solutions for the aviation and tourism industries.

title: Executive VP & Head of Technology and Service Delivery Of Ameriprise Financial Sold 47% Of Their Shares
summary: Gerard Smyth, Executive VP & Head of Technology and Service Delivery at Ameriprise Financial (NYSE:AMP), recently sold US$2.6m worth of shares, reducing his holding by 47%. While this is a significant disposal, a previous large sale by the Chairman & CEO was at a higher price than the current stock value. Despite these insider sales, the company is profitable and growing, though insider ownership is relatively low at 0.2%.

title: PNC Financial Services Group Inc. Decreases Stock Position in BlackRock $BLK
summary: PNC Financial Services Group Inc. reduced its stake in BlackRock (NYSE:BLK) by 1.5% in the fourth quarter, selling 5,375 shares and leaving it with 355,620 shares valued at $380,634,000. Despite this, several other institutional investors increased their positions in BlackRock, and 80.69% of the stock is held by institutional investors. The article also provides recent analyst ratings, insider transaction details, and current stock performance data for BlackRock, noting its consensus "Moderate Buy" rating and a target price of $1,269.06.

title: Northwestern Mutual Wealth Management Co. Buys 170,745 Shares of Northern Trust Corporation $NTRS
summary: Northwestern Mutual Wealth Management Co. significantly increased its stake in Northern Trust Corporation (NTRS) by 1,838.1% in the fourth quarter, purchasing an additional 170,745 shares, bringing its total holding to $22.8 million. This comes as Northern Trust reported strong quarterly results, beating analyst expectations for both EPS and revenue, and announced a quarterly dividend of $0.80 per share. The stock currently holds an average "Hold" rating from analysts with an average price target of $164.08.

title: Lucid’s Top Institutional Holders Added Shares in Q1 Despite New Stock Low: Filing Shows
summary: Despite Lucid Motors' stock hitting a new all-time low in Q1 2026, many of its largest institutional investors, including Vanguard, UBS, BlackRock, and AQR Capital Management, increased their shareholdings. Morgan Stanley was a notable exception, significantly cutting its stake. The aggressive accumulation by most top institutions reflects varied investment strategies amidst the stock's prolonged decline and the company's recent challenges.

title: Gamco Investors INC. ET AL Buys 124,000 Shares of Pitney Bowes Inc. $PBI
summary: Gamco Investors INC. ET AL significantly increased its stake in Pitney Bowes Inc. (PBI) by 33.8% in the fourth quarter, purchasing an additional 124,000 shares, bringing their total holding to 491,200 shares valued at approximately $5.19 million. This move is part of a broader trend of high institutional ownership in Pitney Bowes, with other firms like State Street, Cannell & Spears, Capital Management Corp VA, Invesco, and Dimensional Fund Advisors LP also boosting their positions. Despite mixed analyst sentiment, the stock holds a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with an average price target of $15.18, and Pitney Bowes recently met EPS expectations and raised its dividend to $0.01 per share.

title: Fifth Third Bancorp Has $1.20 Billion Stake in Alphabet Inc. $GOOGL
summary: Fifth Third Bancorp maintains a significant $1.20 billion stake in Alphabet Inc. ($GOOGL), making it their fourth-largest holding despite a 2.5% reduction in the fourth quarter. The article highlights strong institutional support for Alphabet, routine insider sales, and the company's ongoing focus on AI strategy, including a $5 billion AI infrastructure joint venture with Blackstone. It also covers recent analyst price target increases and Alphabet's quarterly dividend boost.

title: Citigroup Inc. $C Shares Purchased by Adell Harriman & Carpenter Inc.
summary: Adell Harriman & Carpenter Inc. increased its stake in Citigroup (NYSE:C) by 12.4% during the fourth quarter, bringing its total holdings to 114,925 shares valued at $13.4 million. This comes as Citigroup reported strong first-quarter results, exceeding revenue and earnings estimates, and announced a $30 billion share buyback program and a $0.60 quarterly dividend. Several other institutional investors also adjusted their positions in Citigroup, which currently has a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts.

title: BlackRock and Vanguard Group disclose holdings in Bonk Treasury Company BNKK stock
summary: BlackRock and Vanguard Group have disclosed small positions in Bonk Treasury Company (BNKK) stock, with BlackRock holding 33,188 shares valued at approximately $87,000, and Vanguard Group holding 188,580 shares valued at approximately $491,000. These holdings likely stem from routine allocations of passive index funds rather than active investment in the "meme coin" related company. Bonk, Inc. previously transformed from Nasdaq-listed Safety Shot, Inc. into a digital asset treasury company.

title: Commerzbank Aktiengesellschaft: Release according to Article 40, Section 1 of the WpHG [the German Securities Trading Act] with the objective of Europe-wide distribution
summary: This article announces that BlackRock, Inc. has notified Commerzbank Aktiengesellschaft of a change in its major holdings, as required by Article 40, Section 1 of the German Securities Trading Act (WpHG). BlackRock's voting rights in Commerzbank have slightly decreased from 5.10% to 5.12% when including both direct shares and instruments, with the threshold being crossed on May 14, 2026. The notification also details the complete chain of controlled undertakings under BlackRock, Inc.

title: BlackRock and Vanguard Group Disclose Holdings in Bonk Treasury Corp (BNKK)
summary: BlackRock and Vanguard Group have disclosed minor holdings in Bonk Treasury Company (BNKK) stock through recent 13F filings. BlackRock holds 33,188 shares valued at approximately $87,000, and Vanguard Group holds 188,580 shares valued at about $491,000. These positions are likely due to routine allocation by passive index funds rather than active investment in the "meme coin."

title: iShares ESG Advanced Total USD Bond Market ETF declares monthly distribution of $0.141313
summary: The iShares ESG Advanced Total USD Bond Market ETF has announced a monthly distribution of $0.141313. This declaration provides investors with details regarding the upcoming payout from the exchange-traded fund.

title: The greatest fixed income investment opportunity in decades?
summary: This article discusses the potential for the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield to reach 6% in the next 18 months, driven by fiscal concerns, higher inflation due to tariffs, and sustained global growth. Such a scenario would create a historic fixed income investment opportunity, offering equity-like returns with less volatility, and significantly impacting asset allocation strategies. Investors are advised to consider a curve steepening bias, inflation-linked bonds, and flexible global strategies to navigate this evolving market.

title: Citi, BlackRock’s HPS Launch €15B Private Credit Program Targeting EMEA
summary: Citigroup Inc. and HPS Investment Partners, part of BlackRock, have launched a €15 billion Private Capital Program to expand access to private financing for corporate and sponsor-owned borrowers across the EMEA region. This five-year program will initially focus on continental Europe and the UK, with plans to extend to the Middle East, offering a broad range of sub-investment-grade debt instruments. The collaboration leverages Citi's origination capabilities and HPS's capital and structuring expertise to provide flexible capital solutions.

title: C Partners With BlackRock's HPS: A Win for Its Private Credit Push?
summary: Citigroup (C) has partnered with HPS Investment Partners, a BlackRock affiliate, to launch a €15-billion private credit program for direct lending in Europe, the Middle East, and Africa. This initiative aims to expand Citigroup's presence in the fast-growing private credit market by leveraging its banking network and HPS's capital and structuring expertise. The collaboration allows Citigroup to offer flexible financing solutions, improve capital efficiency, and generate additional fee income while reflecting the evolving landscape of global credit markets.

title: C Partners With BlackRock's HPS: A Win for Its Private Credit Push?
summary: Citigroup Inc. (C) and HPS Investment Partners, part of BlackRock Inc. (BLK), have launched a €15-billion private credit program to expand direct lending across Europe, the Middle East, and Africa. This five-year initiative aims to leverage Citigroup's network for sourcing opportunities and HPS's capital and expertise, positioning Citigroup strongly in the growing private credit market. The partnership also enables a capital-efficient growth strategy for Citigroup and creates opportunities for additional fee income.

title: Janus Henderson expands active ETF push with seven LSE listings
summary: Janus Henderson has listed seven new active UCITS ETFs on the London Stock Exchange, expanding its actively managed offerings for UK and European investors. These listings, which include GBP-hedged share classes, cover a range of strategies including fixed income and equities, and mark a significant step for traditional active managers embracing the ETF structure. The firm's European active ETF platform has now surpassed $1 billion in assets under management following its recent acquisitions and rebranding efforts.

title: Assessing Brighthouse Financial (BHF) Valuation After Recent Steady Share Performance
summary: Brighthouse Financial (BHF) has seen steady share performance, with its stock closing at US$62.54, giving it a market value near US$3.6 billion. The company is considered modestly undervalued, with a fair value estimate of around $65.50, driven by its growth in innovative retirement income products and new distribution channels. However, this valuation depends on BHF overcoming recent losses and managing capital constraints.

title: Fund Files: SEI,Carlyle file for interval fund; BlackRock plots 2 new muni ETFs
summary: Citywire reports that SEI and Carlyle have filed for an interval fund, while BlackRock is planning to launch two new municipal bond ETFs. This article provides a daily summary of new fund launches, strategy adjustments, and changes in fund management.

title: Bank of America discloses Bitcoin, Ethereum, XRP, and Solana ETF positions
summary: Bank of America has revealed nearly $53 million in crypto ETF holdings as of the first quarter of 2026, demonstrating continued institutional interest in digital assets. The majority of these holdings are in Bitcoin ETFs, particularly BlackRock's IBIT. While Bitcoin exposure increased, the bank showed more caution with Ethereum and Solana ETFs, reducing some positions.

title: SAP SE: Release according to Article 40, Section 1 of the WpHG [the German Securities Trading Act] with the objective of Europe-wide distribution
summary: This article reports a major holdings notification from BlackRock, Inc. regarding SAP SE. BlackRock, Inc. has acquired/disposed of shares with voting rights in SAP SE, crossing a threshold on May 14, 2026, and now holds a total of 6.55% of voting rights. The announcement details BlackRock's direct and indirect voting rights, including those held through various financial instruments, and lists the extensive chain of controlled undertakings under BlackRock.

title: Citi and BlackRock’s HPS team up on private capital programme
summary: Citigroup and HPS Investment Partners, a division of BlackRock, have launched a €15bn private capital program aimed at offering broader financing options for corporate and sponsor-backed borrowers in the EMEA region. This collaboration will leverage Citi's extensive origination network and HPS's capital and structuring expertise to support strategic objectives through tailored private credit solutions. The program is expected to back €15bn in debt deals over five years and builds on previous partnerships between Citi and BlackRock, including asset management and advisory services.

title: Largest asset managers escalate ETF competition as market floods with products
summary: The article highlights the intensifying competition among the largest asset managers in the exchange-traded fund (ETF) market as more products are being introduced. This development suggests a growing importance of ETFs in investment strategies and increased pressure on asset managers to innovate and capture market share. The date of publication is May 19, 2026, at 11:23 AM EDT.

title: Resona Asset Management Co. Ltd. Decreases Holdings in BlackRock $BLK
summary: Resona Asset Management Co. Ltd. reduced its stake in BlackRock (NYSE:BLK) by 3.6% in the fourth quarter, selling 1,711 shares and bringing its total holdings to 45,513 shares valued at $48.865 million. Other institutional investors also adjusted their positions in BlackRock, which recently reported impressive earnings and revenue figures, exceeding analyst expectations. The stock's performance and future outlook are influenced by a mix of positive news, such as new partnerships and potential investments, and negative sentiment stemming from regulatory scrutiny of its private credit funds and decreased inflows into its Bitcoin ETF.

title: Citadel XRP ETF rumor: $1.7m exposure claim sparks trading buzz—but no filings
summary: A rumor is circulating that Citadel gained $1.7 million in exposure to XRP through Bitwise and Canary XRP ETFs, sparking significant interest in crypto markets. Despite the buzz, no official regulatory filings have confirmed Citadel's positions, meaning the market is reacting to an unverified narrative. This speculation highlights growing institutional interest in regulated crypto investment vehicles and XRP's increasing relevance as legal clarity improves around Ripple.

title: Ether Fatigue? Fidelity’s FETH ETF Sees $11 Million Exit Despite Three-Month Crypto Gains
summary: The Fidelity Ethereum Fund ETF (FETH) experienced $11.08 million in outflows on May 18, 2026, slightly reducing its $1.16 billion AUM, despite Ethereum (ETH-USD) gaining 9.4% in the last three months. This outflow suggests profit-taking and investor caution rather than a complete retreat from ether exposure. Short-term sentiment signals a "Sell," creating a divergence from medium-term momentum.

title: ProShare Advisors LLC Decreases Stake in McDonald's Corporation $MCD
summary: ProShare Advisors LLC reduced its holdings in McDonald's Corporation by 3.8% in the fourth quarter, selling over 30,000 shares. Despite this, institutional ownership remains high, with several other major firms increasing their stakes. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating for MCD, with the company recently outperforming earnings and revenue estimates.

title: BlackRock, Inc's long position in H-shares of Shandong Gold Mining decreases on May 13, HKEX filing shows
summary: MarketScreener reported that BlackRock, Inc.'s long position in H-shares of Shandong Gold Mining decreased on May 13, according to an HKEX filing. This news comes amidst other recent reports for Shandong Gold Mining, including a 41% jump in Q1 profit and a 61% rise in 2025 profit. The article also provides detailed company information, financial data, and analyst consensus for Shandong Gold Mining.

title: BofA Doubles Nio Stake in Q1 to Record 14.2 Million Shares
summary: Bank of America significantly increased its stake in Chinese EV manufacturer Nio during the first quarter of 2026, reaching a record 14.2 million shares. This move marks BofA's largest holding in Nio since 2018, despite Nio's stock having lost over 90% of its value since its 2021 peak. Other institutional investors like BlackRock also increased their Nio holdings, while major shareholders like DE Shaw & Co, BNP Paribas, and Jane Street Group reduced their positions.

title: Invesco India ETF (IMVP) Competitors
summary: This article provides a detailed comparison of Invesco India ETF (IMVP) with several of its competitors in the Financial Services sector, analyzing key financial metrics such as market capitalization, price, P/E ratio, and Return on Equity (ROE). It highlights IMVP's role within the sector and assesses its short-term risk and volatility against other funds. The content includes a comparison table with various financial indicators for IMVP and its peer group.

title: Japan’s XRP ETF Push Gains Momentum With SBI Proposal
summary: Japanese financial giant SBI Holdings is proposing new Bitcoin and XRP exchange-traded funds (ETFs) for the Tokyo Stock Exchange, including a dedicated Bitcoin-XRP ETF and a hybrid gold-and-crypto investment trust. This move follows Japan's reclassification of crypto assets as financial instruments, positioning the country as a leader in regulated crypto markets. If approved, these ETFs could significantly boost XRP's liquidity and institutional adoption in Japan.

title: JPMorgan Active Value ETF (JAVA) Competitors
summary: This article provides a detailed comparison of JPMorgan Active Value ETF (JAVA) against numerous competitors within the Financial Services sector. It presents a peer comparison table with fundamental metrics like price, market cap, P/E ratio, EPS, and dividend yield, and then individually compares JAVA to 50 other funds or stocks based on market cap, price, P/E ratios, ROE, and volatility. The analysis indicates that JAVA generally exhibits higher short-term volatility compared to most of its listed competitors.

title: TAG Immobilien AG: Release according to Article 40, Section 1 of the WpHG [the German Securities Trading Act] with the objective of Europe-wide distribution
summary: This announcement by TAG Immobilien AG details a notification of major holdings by BlackRock, Inc., indicating an acquisition/disposal of shares with voting rights. BlackRock, Inc. now holds a total of 6.65% of the voting rights in TAG Immobilien AG, comprising 5.03% through shares and 1.62% through various financial instruments. The notification, dated May 19, 2026, also provides a full chain of controlled undertakings under BlackRock, Inc.

title: AMF Tjanstepension AB Buys 229,654 Shares of Citigroup Inc. $C
summary: AMF Tjanstepension AB increased its stake in Citigroup Inc. by 26.9% in the fourth quarter, purchasing an additional 229,654 shares, bringing its total holdings to over 1 million shares valued at $126.6 million. This increase occurred amidst Citigroup reporting stronger-than-expected quarterly earnings and announcing a $30 billion share buyback program and a $0.60 quarterly dividend. Several analysts have maintained "Buy" or "Overweight" ratings and raised price targets for Citigroup, further suggesting positive sentiment around the stock.

title: Assessing Invesco (IVZ) Valuation After Strong AUM Growth And Upcoming CEO Investor Event
summary: Invesco (IVZ) has seen renewed investor interest following strong monthly Assets Under Management (AUM) growth in April and an upcoming CEO investor event. With the stock trading at $27.39 and showing significant returns, analysts peg its fair value at $29.32, suggesting it's modestly undervalued based on discounted cash flow. The company's expansion into private and alternative assets aligns with market demand and could drive future revenue and earnings.

title: Goldman Sachs cuts XRP and Solana ETF holdings
summary: Goldman Sachs significantly reduced its cryptocurrency ETF exposure in Q1 2026, completely exiting positions in XRP and Solana funds while trimming Bitcoin and Ether ETF holdings. Despite this, the bank maintained substantial Bitcoin ETF exposure and increased investments in crypto-related equities like Circle and Coinbase. The bank's filings with the SEC revealed these shifts in its crypto investment strategy.

title: Yesterday's spot BTC ETF net outflow reached the highest level in nearly two months
summary: US spot Bitcoin ETFs experienced a net outflow of $649 million yesterday, marking the largest single-day outflow since February 23. IBIT and ARKB saw the most significant capital exits, with $448 million and $110 million respectively. This outflow is notable as ETF fund inflows typically show a positive correlation with BTC's price.

title: Goldman Sachs reshuffles crypto ETFs, keeps bitcoin and exits XRP and Solana
summary: Goldman Sachs divested all its holdings in XRP and Solana spot ETFs in Q1, according to its 13F filing. While it also cut some bitcoin and ethereum spot ETF positions, it added a staking-focused ethereum trust. This indicates a strategic shift away from altcoin spot ETFs, maintaining a strong bitcoin presence, and a focus on staking products for ethereum, alongside increased investments in crypto infrastructure companies.

title: Why Is iShares MSCI Global Min Vol Factor ETF (ACWV) Stock Down Today?
summary: The iShares MSCI Global Min Vol Factor ETF (ACWV) is currently up 0.07% at $122.11, despite the article's misleading "down today" headline. The stock is trading above its 50-day and 200-day moving averages, indicating short-term and long-term strength. Forecast models project positive returns over one, three, and five years, with a slight negative return anticipated in one month.

title: Citi, BlackRock’s HPS Add €15bn Direct Lending in EMEA
summary: Citigroup Inc. and HPS Investment Partners (part of BlackRock) have launched a €15 billion Private Capital Program. This strategic collaboration aims to expand comprehensive capital solutions and access to private financing for corporate and sponsor-owned borrowers in the EMEA region over an initial five-year term. Citi will leverage its origination capabilities to source opportunities, while HPS will contribute significant capital and structuring expertise to provide tailored direct lending solutions.

title: Best Crypto ETFs to Buy in 2026
summary: The article highlights the growing institutional adoption of cryptocurrency ETFs, particularly in Bitcoin and Ethereum. It identifies iShares Bitcoin Trust ETF and Fidelity Wise Origin Bitcoin Fund as leading Bitcoin ETFs, and iShares Ethereum Trust ETF for Ethereum exposure, also introducing the iShares Staked Ethereum Trust ETF for staking rewards. The increasing interest from institutional investors suggests a long-term commitment to digital currencies through these accessible investment vehicles.

title: Number of shareholders of BlackRock MuniVest Fund, Inc. – NASDAQ:XMVFX
summary: The article provides information related to the number of shareholders of BlackRock MuniVest Fund, Inc. under the NASDAQ:XMVFX ticker. It includes sections for period value, change, and change percentage, but the specific data is not detailed in the provided content. The page also features general market and financial data services information.
